author: @neoxuehan84
text: Whole video about line shopping and positive ev is wrong. You cannot just only use the de-vigged implied probability because it is mathematically derived from the odds themselves. You need to compare to actual real world probability! Eg. Average, mean, max, min rebounds and freq of the number of rebounds happening. Without comparing de-vigged odds’ probability against real world probability, you are not identifying whether there’s positive ev. Your real world probability needs to be higher than de-vigged probability, that’s what positve ev is about.

author: @Keepitp-k6y
text: Many people misunderstand break-even percentages on PrizePicks. While 5 and 6 pick entries seem more profitable b/c they have lower break-even rates, this overlooks the actual probability of winning. Each individual pick has its own chance of hitting, and when you stack 5 or 6 of them together, the probability of hitting all of them drops significantly. So even though you technically need to win fewer entries to break even, it's much harder to actually hit those long-shot parlays compared to more frequent wins on 3 or 4 pick entries.

author: @MaxiLui-c7z
text:
A fair bet pays you enough to match the risk. If an event has a 30% chance, the fair odds should be 3.33 — but if you're only getting 3.00, you're being underpaid.

To see why this matters:
Winning two 56% bets has a 31.3% chance, and if each pays 1.79, the combined payout is 3.20.
So, a 31.3% event should pay at least 3.20 — not 3.00.

That small gap means you're losing value every time you bet.
In the long run, you'll lose money, because the odds don't fairly match the risk.

author: @zzzTAIzzz
text:
PSA: The “winnings” section that he shows is NOT net winnings, just total! He very well may have wagered 250k to “win” the 200k he shows, which would mean he’s still -50k lifetime. 

Moreover, the “EV” method he speaks of is not only barely profitable (like 2% ROI, not 10) but is a surefire way to get your account limited earlier than necessary. Why? Because there are thousands of other ppl who are betting the exact same stuff as you, who have access to the same screens, etc. etc. when sportsbooks like prizepicks see 100 different wagers on the same play in the span of 5 minutes, assuming the bet is in a niche market (like most “EV” wagers are, such as soccer fouls or free throws made), they know you are likely an advantage player and will thus flag your account immediately, along with everyone else who bet the play. A further flag that comes with doing this is CLV, for the same reason: such a sharp influx of imbalanced wagers will inevitably cause the line you are betting on to move in your favor. This is the NUMBER ONE characteristic of how sportsbooks profile sharp accounts and subsequently limit them, if they are consistently able to generate CLV and beat the market line. Running the “EV” system this guy talks about will generate CLV on literally all of your bets, and much of this will be “synthetic”, meaning the line is moving off of pure volume and not because sharp bettors/the market necessarily agrees with you, since the plays are all originated from the same odds screen. 

In the same vein as above, do NOT ever tail plays from popular “cappers.” Vast majority are lifetime losers and if you somehow manage to find one that isn’t, you will quickly get limited for the same reason as above. If you ever were to tail a capper, make sure they a) have a documented record or vouches of longtime winning and b) have <100 subscribers/regular tailers. Ideally, less than 20 to be honest. 

My credentials: I made a NET profit of $50k from prizepicks alone (on just my account, not including partners) before I was limited. My “leash” was extended because I ORIGINATED everything with my models, mainly for league of legends and NBA props. This meant that I would generate much less CLV than an oddsjam guy, yet have a much better ROI (10% on NBA and like 30% on league). In fact, more often than not I would get negative CLV because a big esports capper would take the opposite side of my play (I.e. the over while I have the under), causing the line to move away from me, then I’d bet it again and win. 

I am also a cofounder of a small betting syndicate which made over $200k last year JUST from DFS, not including actual sportsbooks which had the bulk of the profit. If you are interested in more, dm me on discord: my @ is “gucky.” Yes include the period. No, I’m not trying to sell you anything and won’t ever. Mainly looking for people to help me get down more $$$ but also willing to teach a few ppl the actual ropes of sports betting, not the BS fed in this video

A thing to note, if something is heavily juiced like a -175 over and an under +120, the difference in both is too high (just an example). But if you are solely adding something that is juiced like that to a slip just to win, then take it.
